names.scientific_name: Sodium aluminium silicate (Na2Al2Si2O8 and related forms)
names.japanese_name: アルミノケイ酸ナトリウム

natural_occurrence.description: While sodium aluminium silicate occurs in nature as zeolites and feldspars, food-grade material is synthetically produced for purity and consistent properties.

manufacturing.description: Produced by reacting sodium silicate with aluminum sulfate or aluminum hydroxide, followed by washing, drying, and milling. The synthetic process controls particle size and purity.

other_uses.industrial: Zeolite catalysts, water softening, detergents, molecular sieves.

eu.conditions: Permitted as anti-caking agent. Part of aluminum-containing additives with exposure concerns.

usa.notes: Maximum 2% by weight as anti-caking agent.

japan.japanese_regulation_name: アルミノケイ酸ナトリウム
japan.notes: Restricted use with monitoring of aluminum exposure.

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: not specified (subject to overall aluminum limits)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: no ADI (part of overall aluminum TWI of 1 mg/kg bw/week)

regulatory_history.event: EFSA re-evaluated aluminum-containing food additives including E554, expressing concern about dietary aluminum exposure, particularly for children.

fun_facts: Related to zeolites - minerals with cage-like structures used as molecular sieves and catalysts
One of the aluminum-containing additives under regulatory scrutiny due to aluminum exposure concerns
Extremely effective anti-caking agent due to its porous structure that absorbs moisture
Natural zeolites have been used for water purification for centuries
Part of ongoing debate about aluminum in food and potential health effects
The same family of compounds used in cat litter and water softeners (industrial grade zeolites)
